author: Ben Jacobs
raw_content:
On Mo Salah and Saudi, important to note most clubs can't make their move yet until the 2026/27 seasonal budget is allocated.

Salahâ€™s agent Ramy Abbas has made it clear there is no club yet lined up and serious talks are yet to begin with anyone for this summer.

Saudi dealmakers have been keen on Salah since 2023 when Al-Ittihad made a bid worth Â£100m plus Â£50m in add ons. This was immediately turned down by Mike Gordon via a phone call.

Salah was viewed back then as becoming the poster boy of the new Saudi Pro League project and was offered lucrative terms in the ballpark of Cristiano Ronaldo's salary.

Al-Hilal have also shown a strong interest in the past in Salah, particularly prior to the Egyptian signing his new two year Liverpool contract in April 2025. 

Sources close to the Saudi Pro League reveal they were anticipating paying a fee when the season started and expected to face competition from MLS clubs. 

For Saudi clubs, Salah becoming a free transfer still presents a significant cost via wages, and signing on and agent fees, which most can't shoulder without central support, but the lack of transfer fee could tempt European suitors who would only have done a deal in 2027 previously.

The only way for the majority of Saudi clubs to sign Salah without central funding is to use private investment, which was the case when Al-Hilal signed Karim Benzema with the support of Prince Alwaleed bin Talal bin Abdulaziz. Despite perceptions all Saudi clubs have these kind of investors, very few do at the scale required to land a player like Salah.

The one club that is an exception to this rule or approach is Aramco-owned Al-Qadsiah, who have Brendan Rodgers as their manager and funds in place to do business that is not reliant on Ministry support.

Senior Saudi Pro League sources admit several clubs are interested in Salah but caution against the league itself signing off on a transfer unless a return on the investment can be justified.

League officials wanted to sign Salah last summer to coincide with a new TV rights cycle in the hope of gaining a lucrative deal in Egypt. That ship has now sailed.

Salah is understood to have wanted to reach a mutual agreement with Liverpool to part ways and announce his departure on his own terms prior to formally exploring his next step, and thatâ€™s partly why nothing is lined up.ðŸ‡ªðŸ‡¬

raw_content: LeBron James will make his Los Angeles Lakers season debut against the Utah Jazz tonight at home, sources tell me and @mcten. The four-time NBA champion has rehabbed a nerve issue on his right side and now will take the court for a league-record 23rd season. https://t.co/yvxiI6fZ4w

author: Shams Charania
raw_content: Lakers' LeBron James has been upgraded to questionable for Tuesday's home game against the Utah Jazz, an indication he hopes to make his season debut. James is motivated and eager to return to action, and said at practice Monday that he is monitoring his body around the clock.

author: Shams Charania
raw_content: Cunningham becomes the sixth active NBA player to have a signature shoe deal with Nike, joining LeBron James, Kevin Durant, Ja Morant, Devin Booker and Giannis Antetokounmpo.

author: Shams Charania
raw_content: LeBron James has been reassigned back to the Lakers from NBA G League South Bay and will be a full participant at Lakers practice on Monday, sources tell ESPN. James completed multiple days of practices and 5-on-5 sessions this week in the G League and his debut is nearing.

author: Shams Charania

Breaking: Los Angeles Lakers' LeBron James is opting into his $52.6 million player option for the 2025-26 season, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul told ESPN. 

Paul adds: LeBron "knows the Lakers are building for the future ... but he values a realistic chance of winning it all." https://t.co/q5mT1O2R35

raw_content: After sustaining a groin strain two weeks ago, Lakers star LeBron James will return tonight against the Chicago Bulls.

raw_content: Lakers' LeBron James has returned to Los Angeles, per medical recommendation, as he continues to progress well from a groin strain with the team finishing road trip Thursday in Milwaukee and Friday in Denver, league sources tell ESPN.
